revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''道奥菊多国造'''（みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ）は[[令制国]]での[[陸奥国]]菊多郷（きくたのこおり）、後の[...'
revision.text:
'''道奥菊多国造'''（みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ）は[[令制国]]での[[陸奥国]]菊多郷（きくたのこおり）、後の[[福島県]][[菊田郡]]（旧[[勿来|勿来市]]、現[[いわき市]]）辺りにあった'''菊多国'''を支配した[[国造]]。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の巻十『国造本紀』によれば、[[応神天皇]]の時代、[[茨城国造]]の祖・[[建許侶命]]（たけころのみこと）の子である'''屋主刀禰 '''（やぬしのとね、または屋主乃禰：やぬしのね）を国造に定めたことに始まるとされる。
[[常陸国]]から陸奥国へ分割された経緯を持つ当地は[[大和朝廷の北進]]が始まるまで[[ヤマト王権]]の及ぶ北限の地として[[蝦夷]]と対峙し、[[勿来関]]に先立つ菊多関があったとされる。
=== 氏神 === *[[國魂神社 (いわき市)|國魂神社]]（くにたまじんじゃ） - 菊田郡窪田郷（現在の福島県いわき市勿来町窪田馬場72）に鎮座する、菊多国造が創祀したと伝わる神社。
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:陸奥国]] [[Category:古墳時代]] [[Category:飛鳥時代]] [[Category:福島県の歴史]] [[Category:いわき市]] [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の巻十『国造本紀』によれば、[[応神天皇]]の時代、[[茨城国造]]の祖・[[建許侶命]]（たけころのみこと）の子である'''屋主刀禰 '''（やぬしのとね、または屋主乃禰：やぬしのね）を国造に定めたことに始まるとされる。
[[常陸国]]から陸奥国へ分割された経緯を持つ当地は[[大和朝廷の北進]]が始まるまで[[ヤマト王権]]の及ぶ北限の地として[[蝦夷]]と対峙し、[[勿来関]]に先立つ菊多関があったとされる。

'''道奥菊多国造'''（みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ）は[[令制国]]での[[陸奥国]][[菊多郡|菊多郡（きくたのこおり）]]（後の[[福島県]][[菊田郡]]（旧[[勿来|勿来市]]、現[[いわき市]]）辺りにあった'''菊多国'''を支配した[[国造]]。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の巻10『国造本紀』によれば、[[応神天皇]]の時代、[[茨城国造]]の祖・[[建許侶命]]の子である'''屋主刀禰 '''（やぬしのとね、または屋主乃禰：やぬしのね）を国造に定めたことに始まるとされる。
[[常陸国]]から陸奥国へ分割された経緯を持つ当地は[[大和朝廷の北進]]が始まるまで[[ヤマト王権]]の及ぶ北限の地として[[蝦夷]]と対峙し、[[勿来関]]に先立つ菊多関があったとされる。
== 氏神 == * [[國魂神社 (いわき市)|國魂神社]]（くにたまじんじゃ） - 菊田郡窪田郷（現在の福島県いわき市勿来町窪田馬場72）に鎮座する、菊多国造が創祀したと伝わる神社。
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] **（[[石城国]]：[[浜通り]]）'''菊多関'''（[[勿来関]]）：菊多（菊多郡）- [[石城国造|石城]]（[[磐城郡]]）- [[染羽国造|染羽]]（[[標葉郡]]）- [[浮田国造|浮田]]（[[宇多郡]]）- [[亘国造|亘]]（亘理郡）・[[伊久国造|伊久]]（[[伊具郡]]） **（[[石背国]]：[[中通り]]）'''白川関'''：[[白河国造|白河]]（[[白河郡]]）- [[石背国造|石背]]（[[岩瀬郡]]）- [[阿尺国造|阿尺]]（[[安積郡]]）- [[信夫国造|信夫]]（[[信夫郡]]） **:※ 南から北への順。石背国に属した[[会津郡]]と石城国に属した[[行方郡 (磐城国)|行方郡]]の郡名を負う国造はいない。
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''道奥菊多国造'''（みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ）は、[[令制国]]での[[陸奥国]][[菊多郡|菊多郡（きくたのこおり）]]（後の[[福島県]][[菊田郡]]（旧[[勿来|勿来市]]、現[[いわき市]]南部）にあった'''菊多国'''を支配した[[国造]]。
『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の巻10『国造本紀』によれば、[[応神天皇]]の時代、[[茨城国造]]の祖・[[建許侶命]]の子である'''屋主刀禰 '''（やぬしのとね、または屋主乃禰：やぬしのね）を国造に定めたことに始まるとされる。後に[[多珂国]]（[[日立市|助川]]から[[大熊町|熊川]]まで）の一角に加わった。
[[律令制]]が敷かれた時、当初は[[常陸国]]に編入され、その北限が「苦麻」こと熊川とされたが<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 福島県の地名』205頁</ref>、後に陸奥国へ編入された。この経緯を持つ当地は、[[大和朝廷の北進]]が始まるまで[[ヤマト王権]]の及ぶ北限の地として[[蝦夷]]と対峙し、[[勿来関]]に先立つ菊多関があったとされる。
== 氏神 == * [[國魂神社 (いわき市)|國魂神社]]（くにたまじんじゃ） - 菊田郡窪田郷（現在の福島県いわき市[[勿来町]]窪田馬場72）に鎮座する、菊多国造が創祀したと伝わる神社。
==脚註== <references/>
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] **（[[石城国]]：[[浜通り]]）'''菊多関'''（[[勿来関]]）：菊多（菊多郡）- [[石城国造|石城]]（[[磐城郡]]）- [[染羽国造|染羽]]（[[標葉郡]]）- [[浮田国造|浮田]]（[[宇多郡]]）- [[亘国造|亘]]（[[亘理郡]]）・[[伊久国造|伊久]]（[[伊具郡]]） **（[[石背国]]：[[中通り]]）'''白川関'''：[[白河国造|白河]]（[[白河郡]]）- [[石背国造|石背]]（[[岩瀬郡]]）- [[阿尺国造|阿尺]]（[[安積郡]]）- [[信夫国造|信夫]]（[[信夫郡]]） **:※ 南から北への順。石背国に属した[[会津郡]]と石城国に属した[[行方郡 (磐城国)|行方郡]]の郡名を負う国造はいない。 * [[多珂国]] * [[菊多郡]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

[[律令制]]が敷かれた時、当初は[[常陸国]]に編入され、その北限が「苦麻」こと熊川とされたが<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 福島県の地名』205頁</ref>、後に陸奥国へ編入された。この経緯を持つ当地は、[[大和朝廷の北進]]が始まるまで[[ヤマト王権]]の及ぶ北限の地として[[蝦夷]]と対峙し、[[勿来関]]に先立つ菊多関があったとされる。

revision.text:
'''道奥菊多国造'''（みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ）は、[[令制国]]での[[陸奥国]][[菊多郡|菊多郡（きくたのこおり）]]（後の[[福島県]][[菊田郡]]（旧[[勿来|勿来市]]、現[[いわき市]]南部）にあった'''菊多国'''を支配した[[国造]]。
=== 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の巻10「国造本紀」によれば、[[応神天皇]]の時代、[[茨城国造]]の祖・[[建許侶命]]の子である'''屋主刀禰 '''を国造に定めたことに始まるとされる。
=== 氏族 === '''[[丈部氏]]'''（はせつかべうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）で、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]12年（[[870年]]）に[[菊多郡]]人の丈部継麻呂や丈部浜成ら男女21人が'''湯坐菊多[[臣]]'''を賜姓された。一族には湯坐日理連、宗何部氏などがいる。
[[律令制]]が敷かれた時、当初は[[常陸国]]に編入され、その北限が「苦麻」こと熊川とされたが<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 福島県の地名』205頁</ref>、後に陸奥国へ編入された。この経緯を持つ当地は、[[大和朝廷の北進]]が始まるまで[[ヤマト王権]]の及ぶ北限の地として[[蝦夷]]と対峙し、[[勿来関]]に先立つ菊多関があったとされる。後に[[多珂国]]（[[日立市|助川]]から[[大熊町|熊川]]まで）の一角に加わった。
== 氏神 == * [[國魂神社 (いわき市)|國魂神社]]（くにたまじんじゃ） - 菊田郡窪田郷（現在の福島県いわき市[[勿来町]]窪田馬場72）に鎮座する、菊多国造が創祀したと伝わる神社。

'''道奥菊多国造'''（みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ）は、[[令制国]]での[[陸奥国]][[菊多郡|菊多郡（きくたのこおり）]]（後の[[福島県]][[菊田郡]]（旧[[勿来|勿来市]]、現[[いわき市]]南部）にあった'''菊多国'''を支配した[[国造]]。

国造の領域は[[菊多郡]]を中心とする地域と見られるが、後に[[多珂国]]（[[日立市|助川]]から[[大熊町|熊川]]まで）の一角に加わった。[[律令制]]が敷かれた時、当初は[[常陸国]]に編入され、その北限が「苦麻」こと熊川とされたが<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 福島県の地名』205頁</ref>、後に陸奥国へ編入された。この経緯を持つ当地は、[[大和朝廷の北進]]が始まるまで[[ヤマト王権]]の及ぶ北限の地として[[蝦夷]]と対峙し、[[勿来関]]に先立つ菊多関があったとされる。

== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] **（[[石城国]]：[[浜通り]]）'''菊多関'''（[[勿来関]]）：菊多（菊多郡）- [[石城国造|石城]]（[[磐城郡]]）- [[染羽国造|染羽]]（[[標葉郡]]）- [[浮田国造|浮田]]（[[宇多郡]]）- [[伊久国造|伊久]]（[[伊具郡]]）- [[思国造|思]]（[[志田郡]]） **（[[石背国]]：[[中通り]]）'''白川関'''：[[白河国造|白河]]（[[白河郡]]）- [[石背国造|石背]]（[[岩瀬郡]]）- [[阿尺国造|阿尺]]（[[安積郡]]）- [[信夫国造|信夫]]（[[信夫郡]]） **:※ 南から北への順。石背国に属した[[会津郡]]と石城国に属した[[行方郡 (福島県)|行方郡]]の郡名を負う国造はいない。 * [[菊多郡]] * [[多珂国]] * [[道口岐閉国造]] * [[石城国造]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:陸奥国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]]

revision.comment: 最初の見出しが =3つでかつ以降に =2つの見出し
revision.text:
'''道奥菊多国造'''（みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ）は、[[令制国]]での[[陸奥国]][[菊多郡|菊多郡（きくたのこおり）]]（後の[[福島県]][[菊田郡]]（旧[[勿来|勿来市]]、現[[いわき市]]南部）にあった'''菊多国'''を支配した[[国造]]。
== 祖先 == *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の巻10「国造本紀」によれば、[[応神天皇]]の時代、[[茨城国造]]の祖・[[建許侶命]]の子である'''屋主刀禰 '''を国造に定めたことに始まるとされる。
== 氏族 == '''[[丈部氏]]'''（はせつかべうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は不明）で、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]12年（[[870年]]）に[[菊多郡]]人の丈部継麻呂や丈部浜成ら男女21人が'''湯坐菊多[[臣]]'''を賜姓された。一族には湯坐日理連、宗何部氏などがいる。
国造の領域は[[菊多郡]]を中心とする地域と見られるが、後に[[多珂国]]（[[日立市|助川]]から[[大熊町|熊川]]まで）の一角に加わった。[[律令制]]が敷かれた時、当初は[[常陸国]]に編入され、その北限が「苦麻」こと熊川とされたが<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 福島県の地名』205頁</ref>、後に陸奥国へ編入された。この経緯を持つ当地は、[[大和朝廷の北進]]が始まるまで[[ヤマト王権]]の及ぶ北限の地として[[蝦夷]]と対峙し、[[勿来関]]に先立つ菊多関があったとされる。
== 氏神 == * [[國魂神社 (いわき市)|國魂神社]]（くにたまじんじゃ） - 菊田郡窪田郷（現在の福島県いわき市[[勿来町]]窪田馬場72）に鎮座する、菊多国造が創祀したと伝わる神社。
==脚註== <references/>
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] **（[[石城国]]：[[浜通り]]）'''菊多関'''（[[勿来関]]）：菊多（菊多郡）- [[石城国造|石城]]（[[磐城郡]]）- [[染羽国造|染羽]]（[[標葉郡]]）- [[浮田国造|浮田]]（[[宇多郡]]）- [[伊久国造|伊久]]（[[伊具郡]]）- [[思国造|思]]（[[志田郡]]） **（[[石背国]]：[[中通り]]）'''白川関'''：[[白河国造|白河]]（[[白河郡]]）- [[石背国造|石背]]（[[岩瀬郡]]）- [[阿尺国造|阿尺]]（[[安積郡]]）- [[信夫国造|信夫]]（[[信夫郡]]） **:※ 南から北への順。石背国に属した[[会津郡]]と石城国に属した[[行方郡 (福島県)|行方郡]]の郡名を負う国造はいない。 * [[菊多郡]] * [[多珂国]] * [[道口岐閉国造]] * [[石城国造]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:陸奥国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]]

revision.comment: 領域についての記述を整理。内容には手を入れず。
revision.text:
'''道奥菊多国造'''（みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ）は、'''菊多国'''を支配したとされる[[国造]]。
国造の領域は後の[[菊多郡]]、現在の[[福島県]][[いわき市]]の南部を中心とする地域とみられるが、後に[[多珂国]]（[[日立市|助川]]から[[大熊町|熊川]]まで）の一角に加わった。[[律令制]]が敷かれた時、当初は[[常陸国]]に編入され、その北限が「苦麻」こと熊川とされたが<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 福島県の地名』205頁</ref>、後に[[陸奥国]]へ編入された。この経緯を持つ当地は、[[大和朝廷の北進]]が始まるまで[[ヤマト王権]]の及ぶ北限の地として[[蝦夷]]と対峙し、[[勿来関]]に先立つ菊多関があったとされる。
== 祖先 == 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の巻10「国造本紀」によれば、[[応神天皇]]の時代、[[茨城国造]]の祖・[[建許侶命]]の子である'''屋主刀禰 '''を国造に定めたことに始まるとされる。
== 氏族 == '''[[丈部氏]]'''（はせつかべうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は不明）で、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]12年（[[870年]]）に[[菊多郡]]人の丈部継麻呂や丈部浜成ら男女21人が'''湯坐菊多[[臣]]'''を賜姓された。一族には湯坐日理連、宗何部氏などがいる。
== 氏神 == * [[國魂神社 (いわき市)|國魂神社]]（くにたまじんじゃ） - 菊田郡窪田郷（現在の福島県いわき市[[勿来町]]窪田馬場72）に鎮座する、菊多国造が創祀したと伝わる神社。
==脚註== <references/>
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] **（[[石城国]]：[[浜通り]]）'''菊多関'''（[[勿来関]]）：菊多（菊多郡）- [[石城国造|石城]]（[[磐城郡]]）- [[染羽国造|染羽]]（[[標葉郡]]）- [[浮田国造|浮田]]（[[宇多郡]]）- [[伊久国造|伊久]]（[[伊具郡]]）- [[思国造|思]]（[[志田郡]]） **（[[石背国]]：[[中通り]]）'''白川関'''：[[白河国造|白河]]（[[白河郡]]）- [[石背国造|石背]]（[[岩瀬郡]]）- [[阿尺国造|阿尺]]（[[安積郡]]）- [[信夫国造|信夫]]（[[信夫郡]]） **:※ 南から北への順。石背国に属した[[会津郡]]と石城国に属した[[行方郡 (福島県)|行方郡]]の郡名を負う国造はいない。 * [[菊多郡]] * [[多珂国]] * [[道口岐閉国造]] * [[石城国造]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ** {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ** {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ** Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}
[[ja:道奥菊多国造]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]]

== Notes == <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ** {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ** {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ** Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ** {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ** {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ** Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>

No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

revision.comment: forking the category so massreplace can work better on it, fixing category typo
revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. == Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "陸奥国" to "Mutsu Province "
revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
