== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) * '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) * '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 1]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

== Notes == <references/>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/> == See Also! == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

revision.text:
[[ja:%E9%81%93%E5%A5%A5%E8%8F%8A%E5%A4%9A%E5%9B%BD%E9%80%A0]] '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/> == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}}

No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=draft}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=draft}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=draft}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|12=draft}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=draft}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=draft}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|12=draft}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|12=draft}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国|12=draft}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|12=draft}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=draft}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=draft}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|12=draft}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観|12=draft}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|12=draft}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=draft}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|12=draft}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=draft}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=draft}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=draft}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=draft}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国|12=draft}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|12=draft}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|12=draft}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=draft}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=draft}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=draft}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=draft}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=draft}}

== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|12=draft}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=draft}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=draft}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=draft}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=draft}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国|12=draft}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|12=draft}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|12=draft}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=draft}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=draft}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=draft}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=draft}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=draft}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|12=draft}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=draft}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=draft}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|12=draft}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|12=draft}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国|12=draft}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|12=draft}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=draft}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=draft}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|12=draft}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観|12=draft}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|12=draft}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=draft}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|12=draft}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=draft}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=draft}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=draft}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=draft}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国|12=draft}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|12=draft}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|12=draft}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=draft}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=draft}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=draft}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=draft}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=draft}}

== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|12=draft}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=draft}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=draft}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=draft}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=draft}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国|12=draft}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|12=draft}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|12=draft}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=draft}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=draft}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=draft}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=draft}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''道奥菊多国造'''（みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ）は、'''菊多国'''を支配したとされる[[国造]]。
国造の領域は後の[[菊多郡]]、現在の[[福島県]][[いわき市]]の南部を中心とする地域とみられるが、後に[[多珂国]]（[[日立市|助川]]から[[大熊町|熊川]]まで）の一角に加わった。[[律令制]]が敷かれた時、当初は[[常陸国]]に編入され、その北限が「苦麻」こと熊川とされたが<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 福島県の地名』205頁</ref>、後に[[陸奥国]]へ編入された。この経緯を持つ当地は、[[大和朝廷の北進]]が始まるまで[[ヤマト王権]]の及ぶ北限の地として[[蝦夷]]と対峙し、[[勿来関]]に先立つ菊多関があったとされる。
== 祖先 == 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の巻10「国造本紀」によれば、[[応神天皇]]の時代、[[茨城国造]]の祖・[[建許侶命]]の子である'''屋主刀禰 '''を国造に定めたことに始まるとされる。
== 氏族 == '''[[丈部氏]]'''（はせつかべうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は不明）で、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]12年（[[870年]]）に[[菊多郡]]人の丈部継麻呂や丈部浜成ら男女21人が'''湯坐菊多[[臣]]'''を賜姓された。一族には湯坐日理連、宗何部氏などがいる。
== 氏神 == * [[國魂神社 (いわき市)|國魂神社]]（くにたまじんじゃ） - 菊田郡窪田郷（現在の福島県いわき市[[勿来町]]窪田馬場72）に鎮座する、菊多国造が創祀したと伝わる神社。
==脚註== <references/>
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] **（[[石城国]]：[[浜通り]]）'''菊多関'''（[[勿来関]]）：菊多（菊多郡）- [[石城国造|石城]]（[[磐城郡]]）- [[染羽国造|染羽]]（[[標葉郡]]）- [[浮田国造|浮田]]（[[宇多郡]]）- [[伊久国造|伊久]]（[[伊具郡]]）- [[思国造|思]]（[[志田郡]]） **（[[石背国]]：[[中通り]]）'''白川関'''：[[白河国造|白河]]（[[白河郡]]）- [[石背国造|石背]]（[[岩瀬郡]]）- [[阿尺国造|阿尺]]（[[安積郡]]）- [[信夫国造|信夫]]（[[信夫郡]]） **:※ 南から北への順。石背国に属した[[会津郡]]と石城国に属した[[行方郡 (福島県)|行方郡]]の郡名を負う国造はいない。 * [[菊多郡]] * [[多珂国]] * [[道口岐閉国造]] * [[石城国造]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:陸奥国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]]

'''道奥菊多国造'''（みちのおくのきくたのくにのみやつこ）は、'''菊多国'''を支配したとされる[[国造]]。
国造の領域は後の[[菊多郡]]、現在の[[福島県]][[いわき市]]の南部を中心とする地域とみられるが、後に[[多珂国]]（[[日立市|助川]]から[[大熊町|熊川]]まで）の一角に加わった。[[律令制]]が敷かれた時、当初は[[常陸国]]に編入され、その北限が「苦麻」こと熊川とされたが<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 福島県の地名』205頁</ref>、後に[[陸奥国]]へ編入された。この経緯を持つ当地は、[[大和朝廷の北進]]が始まるまで[[ヤマト王権]]の及ぶ北限の地として[[蝦夷]]と対峙し、[[勿来関]]に先立つ菊多関があったとされる。
== 祖先 == 『[[先代旧事本紀]]』の巻10「国造本紀」によれば、[[応神天皇]]の時代、[[茨城国造]]の祖・[[建許侶命]]の子である'''屋主刀禰 '''を国造に定めたことに始まるとされる。
== 氏族 == '''[[丈部氏]]'''（はせつかべうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は不明）で、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]12年（[[870年]]）に[[菊多郡]]人の丈部継麻呂や丈部浜成ら男女21人が'''湯坐菊多[[臣]]'''を賜姓された。一族には湯坐日理連、宗何部氏などがいる。

== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] **（[[石城国]]：[[浜通り]]）'''菊多関'''（[[勿来関]]）：菊多（菊多郡）- [[石城国造|石城]]（[[磐城郡]]）- [[染羽国造|染羽]]（[[標葉郡]]）- [[浮田国造|浮田]]（[[宇多郡]]）- [[伊久国造|伊久]]（[[伊具郡]]）- [[思国造|思]]（[[志田郡]]） **（[[石背国]]：[[中通り]]）'''白川関'''：[[白河国造|白河]]（[[白河郡]]）- [[石背国造|石背]]（[[岩瀬郡]]）- [[阿尺国造|阿尺]]（[[安積郡]]）- [[信夫国造|信夫]]（[[信夫郡]]） **:※ 南から北への順。石背国に属した[[会津郡]]と石城国に属した[[行方郡 (福島県)|行方郡]]の郡名を負う国造はいない。 * [[菊多郡]] * [[多珂国]] * [[道口岐閉国造]] * [[石城国造]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
'''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|12=draft}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=draft}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|12=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=draft}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|12=draft}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|12=draft}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国|12=draft}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|12=draft}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=draft}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=draft}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|12=draft}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観|12=draft}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|12=draft}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=draft}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|12=draft}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=draft}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=draft}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=draft}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=draft}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国|12=draft}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|12=draft}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|12=draft}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=draft}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=draft}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=draft}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=draft}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=draft}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=draft}}
{{translated page|ja|道奥菊多国造|version=92856823|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki, Fukushima|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|qq=}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|qq=}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō system|qq=}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Province|qq=}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|qq=}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|qq=}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|qq=}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|qq=}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Province|qq=}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Province|qq=}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|qq=}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|qq=}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|qq=}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Province|qq=}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Province|qq=}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|qq=}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|qq=}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove sole member from [[Category:Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko]]
revision.text:
'''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|qq=}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki, Fukushima|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|qq=}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|qq=}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō system|qq=}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Province|qq=}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|qq=}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|qq=}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|qq=}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Jōgan era|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan era|qq=}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|qq=}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} ''' {{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Province|qq=}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Ishitsu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Province|qq=}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|qq=}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|qq=}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
