Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|qq=}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|qq=}}. During the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the region was initially incorporated into [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|qq=}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|qq=}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|qq=}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} ''' [[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]] (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shida District) ''' [[:en:Iwase Province|Ishitsu Province]] (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|qq=}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|qq=}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

'''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]], or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|qq=}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|qq=}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|qq=}}. During the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the region was initially incorporated into [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|qq=}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|qq=}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|qq=}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} ''' [[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]] (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shida District) ''' [[:en:Iwase Province|Ishitsu Province]] (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|qq=}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|qq=}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Iwaki, Fukushima
revision.text:
'''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]], or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道奥菊多国造}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|qq=}}. During the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the region was initially incorporated into [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|qq=}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ''' [[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]] (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) ''' [[:en:Iwase Province|Ishitsu Province]] (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|qq=}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|qq=}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|道奥菊多国造|version=92856823|comment=Imported full ja history}}

No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|qq=}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|qq=}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "paes with no wikidata" to "Pages with no Wikidata"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]], or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道奥菊多国造}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|qq=}}. During the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the region was initially incorporated into [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|qq=}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ''' [[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]] (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) ''' [[:en:Iwase Province|Ishitsu Province]] (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|qq=}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|qq=}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

During the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the region was initially incorporated into [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|qq=}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ''' [[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]] (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) ''' [[:en:Iwase Province|Ishitsu Province]] (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|qq=}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|qq=}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11641674]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]], or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道奥菊多国造|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=Takaku Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|qq=|1=Kumagawa, Ōkuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567884}}. During the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the region was initially incorporated into [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命|1=Takekuro no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486843}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|qq=|1=Hasebe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.

Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|qq=|1=Aizu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381283}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Namekata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626139}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=Takaku Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641650}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

Miyatsuko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|qq=|1=Aizu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381283}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Namekata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626139}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641650}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11641674]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]], or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道奥菊多国造|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|qq=|1=kumakawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567884}}. During the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the region was initially incorporated into [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命|1=Takekuro no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486843}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|qq=|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ''' [[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]] (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) ''' [[:en:Iwase Province|Ishitsu Province]] (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|qq=|1=Aizu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381283}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Namekata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626139}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641650}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|qq=|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|qq=|1=kumakawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567884}}. During the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the region was initially incorporated into [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命|1=Takekuro no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486843}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|qq=|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11641674]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]], or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道奥菊多国造|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|1=kumakawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567884}}. During the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the region was initially incorporated into [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命|1=Takekuro no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486843}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ''' [[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]] (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) ''' [[:en:Iwase Province|Ishitsu Province]] (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|1=Aizu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381283}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|1=Namekata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626139}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641650}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ''' [[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]] (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) ''' [[:en:Iwase Province|Ishitsu Province]] (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|1=Aizu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381283}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|1=Namekata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626139}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641650}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ''' [[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]] (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) ''' [[:en:Iwase Province|Ishitsu Province]] (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|1=Aizu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381283}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|1=Namekata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626139}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641650}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11641674]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]], or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道奥菊多国造|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|1=kumakawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567884}}. During the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the region was initially incorporated into [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命|1=Takekuro no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486843}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ''' [[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]] (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) ''' [[:en:Iwase Province|Ishitsu Province]] (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|1=Aizu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381283}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|1=Namekata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626139}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641650}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|1=Aizu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381283}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|1=Namekata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626139}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641650}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11641674]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]], or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道奥菊多国造|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|1=kumakawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567884}}. During the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], the region was initially incorporated into [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]], with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato court]] before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命|1=Takekuro no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486843}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan era]] (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
