Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|1=kumakawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567884}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命|1=Takekuro no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486843}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.

'''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道奥菊多国造|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|1=kumakawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567884}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命|1=Takekuro no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486843}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.

No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|1=Aizu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381283}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|1=Namekata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626139}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641650}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]]
'''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道奥菊多国造|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|1=kumakawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567884}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命|1=Takekuro no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486843}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ''' {{ill|Iwaki Province (718)|en|Iwaki Province Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Iwase Province|en|Iwase Province|WD=Q1166897|lt=Ishitsu Province}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|1=Aizu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381283}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|1=Namekata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626139}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641650}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]]
'''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道奥菊多国造|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|1=kumakawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567884}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court|1=Yamato Kingship}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命|1=Takekoro-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486843}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Q11617300|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era|1=Zhenguan}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=Kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ''' {{ill|Iwaki Province (718)|en|Iwaki Province (718)|WD=Q1192809|lt=Ishikami Province|1=Iwaki Province (718)}} (Hamadori Region) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' (菊多関): Kikuta (Kikuta District) → {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwakiyama District) → {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hyouge District) → {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) → {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Q85876227|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) → {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) ''' {{ill|Iwase Province|en|Iwase Province|WD=Q1166897|lt=Ishitsu Province|1=Iwase Province}} (Nakadori Region) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''' (白川関): {{ill|Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|白河国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shirakawa District) → {{ill|Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Q11587159|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishitsu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) → {{ill|Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashina Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Q11657392|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) → {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Q11386035|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ''' Note: Ordered from south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|1=Aizu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381283}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|1=Namekata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626139}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619613}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11668988}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Q11641650|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641650}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court|1=Yamato Kingship}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命|1=Takekoro-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486843}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Q11617300|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era|1=Zhenguan}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=Kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:道奥菊多国造]]
'''Michinooku no Kikuta no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (道奥菊多国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|qid=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}, or regional governor, said to have governed the region of {{ill|Kikuta Province|ja|菊多国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道奥菊多国造|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11641674}}.
The domain of this Kuni no Miyatsuko is believed to have corresponded to the later {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11619613}}, an area centered in the southern part of what is now {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|qid=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, Fukushima Prefecture. Later, it became part of {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11668988}}, which stretched from {{ill|Sukegawa|ja|助川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukegawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to {{ill|Kumagawa, Ōkuma|ja|熊川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumagawa, Ōkuma|1=kumakawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11567884}}. During the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, the region was initially incorporated into {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}, with its northern boundary marked by "Kuma" (Kumagawa).<ref>''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Fukushima no Chimei,'' p. 205.</ref> Subsequently, it was reassigned to {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|qid=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}. Due to this historical trajectory, the area was regarded as the northernmost territory under the control of the {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato court|1=Yamato Kingship}} before its northern expansion and was said to host {{ill|Kikuta Seki|ja|菊多関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta Seki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a precursor to the more famous {{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400650}}.
== Ancestry == According to the "Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi" chapter of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, the position of Kuni no Miyatsuko originated during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} when '''Yashiro Tōne''', a son of {{ill|Takekuro no Mikoto|ja|建許侶命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takekuro no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建許呂命|1=Takekoro-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486843}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Q11617300|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11617300}}, was appointed as the first Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Clan == The {{ill|Hasebe clan|ja|丈部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasebe clan|1=hasetsukabeuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55530004}} (はせつかべうじ, kabane unknown) was associated with the Kikuta no Miyatsuko. In the 12th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan era|1=Zhenguan}} (870 CE), members of the Kikuta District, including Hasebe Tsugimaro and Hasebe Hamanari, were granted the surname of {{ill|Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja|湯坐菊多臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yumoriza Kikuta no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Other related families include the Yumoriza Hiratsune no Ren and the Munewakabe clan.
== Shrine == * {{ill|Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja|國魂神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama Shrine (Iwaki)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国魂|1=Kunitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9692242}} – Located in Kuboita, Motonaka-chō, Iwaki City, Fukushima Prefecture, this shrine is said to have been founded by the Kikuta no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == <references/>
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ''' {{ill|Iwaki Province (718)|en|Iwaki Province No Kuni no Miyatsuko is recorded for the {{ill|Aizu District|ja|会津郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aizu District|1=Aizu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11381283}} (Ishitsu Province) or {{ill|Namekata District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡 (福島県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namekata District, Fukushima|1=Namekata district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626139}} (Ishikami Province). * {{ill|Kikuta District|ja|菊多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuta District|1=Kikuta district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11619613}} * {{ill|Takaku Province|ja|多珂国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 高国造|1=takanokunimiyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11668988}} * {{ill|Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道口岐閉国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitchiguchi Kibashi Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Q11641650|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11641650}} * {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
